title: Panel – Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research? – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: The official program and event announcement for the 2026 International Conference on Social Media & Society (#SMSociety 2026) in Glasgow. The announcement lists Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist for a roundtable focused on data transparency, discussing researchers' experiences and challenges using the Meta Content Library (MCL).
description: Fabio Giglietto is featured as a panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable' at the International Conference on Social Media & Society 2026.

title: socialmediaandsociety.org
description: An announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist discussing future pathways for social media research following the closure of CrowdTangle.

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Press coverage reporting on the political ripples of Giglietto's 'A Pretty Blunt Approach' study, which examines algorithmic moderation. The piece notes that his research became the focus of an investigative episode of 'Report' on Rai3 and prompted formal inquiries in the European Parliament.
description: Italian press coverage highlighting the political and societal impact of Fabio Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithmic moderation, noting its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE
description: News article from Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: An Italian news article detailing a major research study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino Carlo Bo [3.1.2]. By analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts from 2021 to 2025, Giglietto's team revealed that Meta's content reduction algorithms severely reduced the reach of mainstream institutional politicians (down up to 73.4% compared to 2021) while inadvertently boosting or failing to suppress extremist and eurosceptic accounts.
description: An Italian news article from Open.online reporting on a major study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which analyzes how Facebook's algorithms impact the visibility of mainstream politicians compared to extremist accounts.
